name: Smart document crop
summary: Detect, crop and auto-orient receipts, invoices and documents from larger images before OCR.
description: Provides smartcrop:// routes for conservative document/receipt detection, cropping and orientation correction. By default it crops from text-line boxes detected by a trained model (PaddleOCR, with Tesseract word boxes as fallback), expands those boundaries to the paper component detected from background contrast using a scale-invariant text-line-height margin, and keeps the full frame when the document is already tightly framed. It falls back to a geometric cascade (OpenCV contour/perspective, bright-sheet fill-ratio, text-region and connected-component thresholds) when no usable text is present. The connector preserves the original image, returns structured crop + orientation metadata, and is designed to run before ocr:// so OCR engines see a portrait-oriented document with horizontal text lines rather than the full camera frame.

payloadOptions.auto_orient: Default true. Rotate the cropped document so text lines are horizontal.
payloadOptions.prefer_portrait: Default true. Prefer portrait output, useful for receipts and invoices.
payloadOptions.margin_ratio: Padding around the detected document before saving.
payloadOptions.fail_if_uncertain: On crop route, fail instead of returning the original when detection is uncertain.
payloadOptions.use_text_boundary: Default true. Crop from detected text boundaries, expand to the background-contrast paper component, then fall back to geometry when unavailable.
payloadOptions.text_boundary_backend: Default auto (prefers the trained PaddleOCR detector, falls back to Tesseract). Supported: auto, paddleocr, tesseract, none/off. Unsupported values fall back to geometry with metadata rather than failing the whole crop.
